channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Sepsis a threat in pope's pneumonia battle as Vatican Holy Year celebrations march on without him
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Vatican carried on with its Holy Year celebrations without the pope Saturday, as Pope Francis battled pneumonia and a complex respiratory infection that doctors say remains touch-and-go and will keep him hospitalized for at least another week.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Carolina death row inmate chooses firing squad as execution method
item.description: A South Carolina death row inmate has chosen to be executed by a firing squad next month rather than by lethal injection or the electric chair.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A mother and her young sons are among the remains returned to Israel by Hamas
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The remains of a mother of two young boys who were taken to Gaza together at the start of the Israel-Hamas war have been returned to Israel, bringing the total closer to the 33 hostages supposed to be released under the ceasefire that started last month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Murder charge issued in death of Michigan 17-year-old
item.description: A murder charge has been filed in the death of a 17-year-old at a Holland, Michigan, apartment early Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Corinne Moore, NewsNation

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is being buried with military honors
item.description: A U.S. Border Patrol agent who was killed in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border is scheduled to be buried at a national cemetery in Minneapolis with full military honors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Parents and kids navigate talks of loss and tragedy as they return home after LA area fires
item.description: When 11-year-old Ceiba Phillips returned to his Southern California neighborhood a month after the devastating Eaton fire, he couldn’t believe his eyes.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know as Michigan Republicans select new chair to lead party into the 2026 midterms
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Michigan Republicans are gathering in Detroit on Saturday to elect a new chair who will lead the party into the 2026 midterms in the battleground state, when several key races will be decided including a critical U.S. Senate seat.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Prosecutors: Presidential pardon for Jan. 6 does not apply to Maryville man's FBI ‘kill list’ plot conviction
item.description: Prosecutors have responded to a motion filed by a Maryville man to dismiss his conviction in to a 2022 plot to murder federal investigators at the FBI Knoxville Field Office after he was pardoned for his role in the January 6, 2021 insurrection at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday canceled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court about the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge upholds ban on DOGE accessing sensitive Treasury information, for now
item.description: A federal judge in Manhattan has extended a ban on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency accessing sensitive Treasury Department information, but rejected broader restrictions sought by 19 Democratic state attorneys general who sued over the issue.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The justices said in an unsigned order that Hampton Dellinger, head of the Office of Special Counsel, could remain in his job at least until Feb. 26. That’s when a lower-court order temporarily protecting him expires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Trump signals he’s open to multiple budget bills instead of just one
item.description: Hours after the Senate approved a spending framework, President Donald Trump said in a Fox News interview that he’s open to approving the spending plan in multiple bills “as long as we get to the same point.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he may take control of the US Postal Service. Here's what to know
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said he may put the U.S. Postal Service under the control of the Commerce Department in what would be an executive branch takeover of the agency, which has operated as an independent entity since 1970.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AP sues 3 Trump administration officials, citing freedom of speech
item.description: The Associated Press is suing three Trump administration officials over access to presidential events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles DA gives update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney said Friday he hasn't yet made a decision on whether Lyle and Erik Menendez should be resentenced in their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Residents in GOP-leaning states concerned about DOGE
item.description: One person asked their lawmaker why DOGE cuts were being “jammed down the pipe so rushed and sloppily.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: SS United States to embark on final voyage to Florida
item.description: A historic ocean liner departed on Thursday for her final voyage, where it heads towards its watery grave off of Florida's coast.
item.dc:creator: Madie MacDonald, NewsNation

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: A federal judge has adjourned the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court on how to handle the Justice Department's request to drop charges against the Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Experienced workers, not just rookies, get cut as Trump slashes probationary employees
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Warren Hill spent more than two decades working at the Lake Clark National Park and Preserve, which spans 4 million acres of coastline, forests, lakes and glaciers in Alaska.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows barefoot foster child with no coat left in snow
item.description: A man in Haskell, Oklahoma, called 911 after hearing a child outside in the cold begging to go back inside a residence. Rhet Wike also captured video of the little girl standing outside in record-breaking freezing temperatures and wearing only pajamas.
item.dc:creator: Heather Holeman/KFOR, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration looks to slash HUD workers tackling the housing crisis
item.description: The Trump administration's proposal to cut half of federal workers at the nation's housing agency is targeting employees who support disaster recovery, rental subsidies, discrimination investigations and first-time homebuyers, according to two documents obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: SALLY HO and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court clears the way for a lawsuit over COVID-19 pandemic-era unemployment claims in Alabama
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — People left waiting for months on their unemployment claims during the coronavirus pandemic in Alabama must be able to sue the state over the delay, the U.S. Supreme Court said Friday. The 5-4 ruling comes after the Alabama Supreme Court rejected a lawsuit from 21 people.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Grand jury recommends Alabama police department be abolished
item.description: The small Alabama city of Hanceville, located about 45 miles north of Birmingham, placed its entire police force on administrative leave a day after the grand jury recommendation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, ABC 4

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Fort Knox's gold depository
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — President Donald Trump says Elon Musk will be looking at Fort Knox, the legendary depository in Kentucky for American gold reserves, to make sure the gold is still there.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA REYNOLDS The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Viral video shows Indiana police punch man during arrest. Here's what we know
item.description: William Jerome Armstrong was arrested Tuesday afternoon in the parking lot of a Walmart after police say they saw his car parked in an emergency fire lane.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, Fox 5

item.title: Tens of millions of dead people aren't getting Social Security checks, despite Trump and Musk claims
item.description: The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order to expand White House control of independent agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aiming to expand the White House’s authority over various independent regulatory agencies, a move likely to prompt legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Father-son duo survives night in Utah wilderness after finding 'miracle' backpack
item.description: In January, a 15-year-old boy was rescued from a southern Utah trail, but left his backpack behind. Weeks later, a father and son found that backpack and used it to survive.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, Fox 5

item.title: 3 victims killed in Green River Tunnel crash identified, 18 people treated for injuries
item.description: The three people who died in a fiery crash in Wyoming's Green River Tunnel on Friday, Feb. 14 have been identified by officials.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Dozens of police recruits nationwide have died while training in last decade: probe
item.description: At least 29 recruits died during basic training at law enforcement academies around the country in the last decade, an AP investigation found.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Democratic governors diverge on how to handle Trump
item.description: Democratic governors are diverging in their approaches to President Trump amid mounting speculation that some of them are gunning for the White House in 2028. Some are digging their heels into resistance efforts, as in Illinois, where Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9
